articles.title: Infosys wins $2.2B US federal cloud modernization contract despite DOGE spending cuts
articles.summary: Infosys has secured a $2.2 billion multi-year contract to modernize cloud infrastructure for three US federal agencies, defying broader DOGE-driven IT spending reductions by positioning the engagement as efficiency-focused rather than expansion-oriented.

articles.title: TCS Q3 FY2026 results: Revenue ₹63,847 crore, AI deal pipeline crosses $4B
articles.summary: Tata Consultancy Services reported Q3 FY2026 revenue of ₹63,847 crore (up 5.6% YoY), with net profit of ₹12,380 crore. CEO K Krithivasan highlighted a $4 billion generative AI deal pipeline, with 12 deals over $100M signed in the quarter.

articles.title: Wipro launches 'AI360' platform: 30,000 employees AI-certified in 6 months
articles.summary: Wipro has launched its enterprise AI transformation platform AI360, certifying 30,000 employees in AI skills in 6 months. The company is repositioning from traditional IT services to AI-led transformation, targeting a 40% reduction in delivery costs through AI automation.

articles.title: HCL Technologies acquires German SAP consultancy for €380M to strengthen EU footprint
articles.summary: HCL Technologies has announced the acquisition of Munich-based SAP consultancy firm Capgemini Germany's enterprise solutions division for €380 million, significantly expanding its SAP S/4HANA implementation capabilities across the European market.

articles.title: EU AI Act compliance deadline 2026: Indian IT companies face $500M opportunity
articles.summary: The European Union's AI Act enforcement begins in phases through 2026, creating mandatory compliance requirements for enterprise AI deployments. Indian IT companies Infosys, TCS, Wipro, and HCL are positioning themselves as preferred compliance implementation partners for their European clients.

articles.title: DOGE federal tech spending cuts: Indian IT companies recalibrate US revenue outlook
articles.summary: The Department of Government Efficiency's sweeping federal technology contract reviews have put $8 billion in active Indian IT company contracts under scrutiny. TCS, Infosys, Wipro, and HCL Technologies are collectively hedging by accelerating private sector and European pipeline development.

articles.title: Generative AI services demand surge: Infosys reports 180% growth in AI revenue
articles.summary: Infosys CEO Salil Parekh reported 180% year-on-year growth in generative AI-related revenue in Q3 FY2026, with the company's AI-first services unit Infosys Topaz now handling 340 active enterprise GenAI projects globally.

articles.title: TCS signs 5-year $800M cloud transformation deal with European insurance giant
articles.summary: TCS has signed an $800 million, 5-year cloud transformation contract with a major European insurance company, covering core system modernization, AI-powered claims processing, and cloud migration using TCS BaNCS and AWS.

articles.title: Wipro Q3 FY2026: Revenue $2.63B, AI-led deals up 65% quarter-on-quarter
articles.summary: Wipro reported Q3 FY2026 revenue of $2.63 billion (flat YoY) with IT services margin improving 80bps to 16.1%. CEO Srinivas Pallia highlighted a 65% sequential increase in AI-led deal wins as the company's AI360 transformation strategy begins to show results.

articles.title: HCL Technologies reports strong Q3: 7.5% revenue growth, GenAI services cross $1B ARR
articles.summary: HCL Technologies reported Q3 FY2026 revenue of ₹30,349 crore, up 7.5% YoY, with net profit of ₹4,591 crore. The company's generative AI services business crossed $1 billion in annual recurring revenue, driven by HCLSOFTWARE and enterprise AI platforms.

articles.title: Indian IT: BFSI cloud migration demand drives $12B deal wins in first 9 months FY2026
articles.summary: The BFSI sector's accelerated core banking and insurance system migration to cloud has driven $12 billion in deal wins for Indian IT companies in the first three quarters of FY2026, with TCS, Infosys, and HCL Technologies collectively capturing 67% of the market.

articles.title: Infosys Topaz GenAI: 340 active projects, Microsoft Azure partnership deepens
articles.summary: Infosys has deepened its partnership with Microsoft Azure for the delivery of Infosys Topaz generative AI solutions, with the companies co-developing industry-specific GenAI accelerators for healthcare, BFSI, and manufacturing clients.
